InstituteProfile.title: InstituteProfile
InstituteProfile.description: Stores comprehensive information about the Maharana Pratap ITI, including contact details, messages from leadership, and general institute descriptions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InstituteProfile entity. (Primary Key)

name.description: The full name of the Industrial Training Institute (ITI), e.g., 'Maharana Pratap ITI Saharanpur Uttar Pradesh'.

description.description: A detailed description providing information about the ITI's mission, history, and facilities.

logoUrl.description: URL to the ITI's official logo image.

mainPhotoUrl.description: URL to the main photograph of the ITI, often displayed on the homepage.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the ITI's physical address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the ITI's physical address (optional).

city.description: The city where the ITI is located.

district.description: The district where the ITI is located.

state.description: The state where the ITI is located.

pincode.description: The postal code of the ITI's location.

locationMapUrl.description: URL to an online map showing the ITI's location.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact telephone number for the ITI.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email address for the ITI.

chairmanName.description: The full name of the ITI's Chairman.

chairmanMessage.description: A welcome or inspirational message from the ITI's Chairman for public display.

chairmanPhotoUrl.description: URL to the photograph of the ITI's Chairman.

principalName.description: The full name of the ITI's Principal.

principalMessage.description: A welcome or informational message from the ITI's Principal for public display.

principalPhotoUrl.description: URL to the photograph of the ITI's Principal.

websiteUrlSlug.description: The URL slug or identifier for the ITI's website, e.g., 'mpitisre'.

Trade.title: Trade
Trade.description: Defines the various industrial training courses or trades offered by the ITI, including their duration and syllabus.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Trade entity. (Primary Key)

name.description: The name of the trade, e.g., 'Electrician', 'Fitter', 'Computer Operator and Programming Assistant'.

durationYears.description: The standard duration of the trade course in years (e.g., 1 or 2).

description.description: A brief description of what the trade entails and its learning objectives.

syllabusDocumentUrl.description: URL to the official syllabus document (e.g., PDF) for this trade.

StudentProfile.title: StudentProfile
StudentProfile.description: Stores comprehensive personal and academic details for approved and currently enrolled students.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentProfile entity. (Primary Key)

email.description: The student's email address, often used for login.

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL to the student's profile photograph, used for ID cards and dashboards.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the student's permanent address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the student's permanent address (optional).

city.description: The city of the student's permanent address.

district.description: The district of the student's permanent address.

state.description: The state of the student's permanent address.

pincode.description: The postal code of the student's permanent address.

tehsil.description: The tehsil (sub-district) of the student's permanent address.

postOffice.description: The post office serving the student's permanent address.

policeStation.description: The police station jurisdiction for the student's permanent address.

tradeId.description: Reference to the Trade entity representing the course the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: Trade 1:N StudentProfile)

sessionYear.description: The academic session/batch the student belongs to, e.g., '2023-2025'.

currentStudyYear.description: The current year of study for the student within their trade, e.g., 'First Year', 'Second Year'.

rollNumber.description: The unique roll number assigned to the student by the ITI.

admissionDate.description: The date when the student's admission was officially finalized.

status.description: The current enrollment status of the student, e.g., 'Approved', 'Active', 'Graduated'.

AdmissionApplication.title: AdmissionApplication
AdmissionApplication.description: Captures details of prospective students who have submitted an online admission form, pending administrative review and approval.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdmissionApplication entity. (Primary Key)

applicantPhotoUrl.description: URL to the photograph uploaded by the applicant.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the applicant's permanent address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the applicant's permanent address (optional).

city.description: The city of the applicant's permanent address.

district.description: The district of the applicant's permanent address.

state.description: The state of the applicant's permanent address.

pincode.description: The postal code of the applicant's permanent address.

tehsil.description: The tehsil (sub-district) of the applicant's permanent address.

postOffice.description: The post office serving the applicant's permanent address.

policeStation.description: The police station jurisdiction for the applicant's permanent address.

applyingTradeId.description: Reference to the Trade entity for which the applicant is applying. (Relationship: Trade 1:N AdmissionApplication)

applyingSessionYear.description: The academic session/batch the applicant is applying for, e.g., '2024-2026'.

applicationDate.description: The date and time when the admission form was submitted.

status.description: The current status of the application (e.g., 'Pending', 'Approved', 'Rejected').

adminComments.description: Notes or comments added by the administrator during the review process (optional).

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Details of assignments created by administrators for specific trades and academic years.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assignment entity. (Primary Key)

description.description: A detailed description or instructions for the assignment.

tradeId.description: Reference to the Trade entity to which this assignment belongs. (Relationship: Trade 1:N Assignment)

applicableYear.description: The academic year for which the assignment is intended (e.g., 'First Year', 'Second Year').

dueDate.description: The deadline for submitting the assignment.

creationDate.description: The date and time when the assignment was created.

assignmentDocumentUrl.description: URL to the assignment document (e.g., PDF containing questions).

StudentAssignmentSubmission.title: StudentAssignmentSubmission
StudentAssignmentSubmission.description: Records a student's submission for a particular assignment, including the submitted work and optional grading/feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentAssignmentSubmission entity. (Primary Key)

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment entity this submission is for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N StudentAssignmentSubmission)

studentId.description: Reference to the StudentProfile entity who made this submission. (Relationship: StudentProfile 1:N StudentAssignmentSubmission)

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the assignment was submitted by the student.

submissionDocumentUrl.description: URL to the student's submitted assignment file or document.

grade.description: The grade or score received by the student for this assignment (optional).

feedbackText.description: Textual feedback provided by the instructor for the submission (optional).

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Stores general announcements and messages to be displayed to students, optionally filtered by trade or academic year.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity. (Primary Key)

title.description: The title or subject of the notification.

content.description: The full text content of the notification.

publicationDate.description: The date and time when the notification was published.

targetTradeIds.description: An array of Trade IDs to which this notification is targeted. An empty array or specific value implies it's for all trades. (Relationship: Trade N:N Notification)

targetStudyYear.description: The academic year(s) for which the notification is intended (e.g., 'First Year', 'Second Year', or 'All').

FeePayment.title: FeePayment
FeePayment.description: Records details of fee payments made by students, including amount, date, and transaction information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FeePayment entity. (Primary Key)

studentId.description: Reference to the StudentProfile entity who made the payment. (Relationship: StudentProfile 1:N FeePayment)

amountPaid.description: The amount of fee paid by the student.

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was made.

transactionReference.description: A unique reference ID from the payment gateway or bank transaction.

feeCategory.description: The category of the fee being paid (e.g., 'Admission Fee', 'Tuition Fee - 1st Year', 'Exam Fee', 'Miscellaneous').

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'Online', 'Cash', 'Bank Transfer').

receiptUrl.description: URL to a digital receipt or proof of payment (optional).

MockTest.title: MockTest
MockTest.description: Information about mock tests provided to students, including links to external test platforms.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MockTest entity. (Primary Key)

title.description: The title of the mock test.

description.description: A brief description or instructions for the mock test.

testPlatformUrl.description: The URL to the external platform where the mock test is hosted (e.g., Google Forms, another quiz platform).

tradeId.description: Reference to the Trade entity for which this mock test is relevant. (Relationship: Trade 1:N MockTest)

applicableYear.description: The academic year for which the mock test is intended (e.g., 'First Year', 'Second Year').

creationDate.description: The date and time when the mock test was created or linked.

StudentTestimonial.title: StudentTestimonial
StudentTestimonial.description: Collects and stores feedback or testimonials from students for display on the public website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentTestimonial entity. (Primary Key)

studentName.description: The name of the student who provided the testimonial.

tradeName.description: The name of the trade the student studied, for context with their testimonial.

testimonialPhotoUrl.description: URL to the student's photo to be displayed alongside their testimonial.

feedbackContent.description: The actual text content of the student's feedback or testimonial.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the testimonial was submitted.

isApproved.description: A boolean indicating whether the testimonial has been approved by an administrator for public display.

definition.description: Stores the single, main profile document for the ITI. Publicly readable, admin writeable.
Collection of industrial training courses offered by the ITI. Publicly readable, admin writeable.

params.name: tradeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific trade course.

definition.description: Stores comprehensive personal and academic details for approved students. Access is restricted to the student owner (request.auth.uid matches studentId) and administrators. The 'id' field in the document MUST match the 'studentId' wildcard.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the student who owns this profile.

definition.description: Captures details of prospective students' admission forms. Public users can create new applications. The document includes a denormalized 'applicantUid' field for owner-specific read/update access by the applicant (matching request.auth.uid). Administrators have full access.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an admission application.

definition.description: Stores details of assignments created by administrators. All authenticated students can read these assignments. Client-side filtering can apply based on 'tradeId' and 'applicableYear'. Admin writeable.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific assignment.

definition.description: Records a student's submission for an assignment. Access is restricted to the student owner (request.auth.uid matches studentId) and administrators. The 'studentId' field in the document MUST match the path's 'studentId' wildcard.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the student making the submission.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific assignment submission.

definition.description: Stores general announcements and messages. All authenticated students can read these notifications. Filtering by 'targetTradeIds' and 'targetStudyYear' is handled client-side. Admin writeable.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific notification.

definition.description: Records details of fee payments made by students. Access is restricted to the student owner (request.auth.uid matches studentId) and administrators. The 'studentId' field in the document MUST match the path's 'studentId' wildcard.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the student making the payment.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific fee payment record.

definition.description: Stores information about mock tests. All authenticated students can read these mock tests. Client-side filtering can apply based on 'tradeId' and 'applicableYear'. Admin writeable.

params.name: mockTestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific mock test.

definition.description: Stores student testimonials awaiting administrative approval ('isApproved' is false). Access is restricted to the student owner (request.auth.uid matches studentId) and administrators. On approval, relevant data is copied to '/publicTestimonials'. The 'studentName' field should match the student's name, and 'studentId' field should implicitly match path's 'studentId' wildcard for ownership.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the student submitting the testimonial.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific student testimonial submission.

definition.description: Stores approved student testimonials for public display ('isApproved' is true). Publicly readable, admin writeable. Data is copied here upon approval from a submission.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: The unique identifier for an approved student testimonial.

definition.description: Dedicated collection to store administrators. The existence of a document for a given UID indicates admin privileges. Used for DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control).

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES, particularly prioritizing Authorization Independence (no `get()` calls in security rules for authorization) and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & DBAC:**
1.  **User-Owned Data:** For entities like `StudentProfile`, `StudentAssignmentSubmission`, `FeePayment`, and `StudentTestimonialSubmission`, data is structured under `/users/{userId}/...` paths (e.g., `/users/{studentId}/studentProfile`). This makes the `userId` (which corresponds to `request.auth.uid`) explicit in the path, allowing rules to authorize access solely based on `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing any `get()` operations. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies debugging.
2.  **Admission Applications:** `AdmissionApplication` documents are placed in a top-level collection `/admissionApplications/{applicationId}`. Each document includes an `applicantUid` field, which is denormalized directly into the document. Security rules can then check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.applicantUid` for applicant-specific access, again avoiding `get()` calls.
3.  **Global Roles (Admin):** Admin roles are managed in a dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`. An administrator's `uid` will correspond to a document in this collection. Security rules can perform an `exists()` check (`resource:get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) for admin verification. This adheres to the DBAC principle.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**
1.  **Public Data:** Publicly accessible information like `InstituteProfile` (a single document `instituteProfile/main`), `Trade` details (`/trades/{tradeId}`), and `PublicTestimonials` (`/publicTestimonials/{testimonialId}`) are placed in dedicated top-level collections. This allows for simple security rules (e.g., `allow read: true;`) that enable secure `list` operations without requiring complex filtering logic in rules. All documents in these collections share the same public read security posture.
2.  **Authenticated Data with Client-Side Filtering:** For `Notification` (`/notifications/{notificationId}`) and `MockTest` (`/mockTests/{mockTestId}`), strict adherence to Authorization Independence implies that server-side rules cannot perform `get()` calls to determine a student's trade or study year for filtering. Therefore, these collections are designed for authenticated users to read all documents (`allow read: if request.auth.uid != null;`). Filtering by `targetTradeIds` or `applicableYear` is expected to be handled client-side. This ensures `list` operations remain performant and secure from a data access perspective, while deferring content relevance to the client.
3.  **Segregation of Testimonials:** The `StudentTestimonial` entity is split into two collections based on its approval status: `/users/{studentId}/testimonialSubmissions/{submissionId}` for pending, private submissions by students, and `/publicTestimonials/{testimonialId}` for approved, publicly displayed testimonials. This ensures each collection has a homogeneous security posture and simplifies rules.

**Overall Structure:**
*   **Readability & Maintainability:** The structure uses descriptive collection and parameter names (e.g., `studentId`, `tradeId`, `applicationId`), making the data organization clear and understandable. This directly aids in writing and debugging security rules.
*   **Scalability:** By avoiding `get()` calls in rules, the system reduces cross-document dependencies, which improves the performance and reliability of atomic operations (transactions, batched writes) and simplifies concurrent access management.
